text.title: Rust Backend (Axum) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules for rust-backend. Each rule pairs binary check + sourced rationale. Last rule is the terminal skip-this-methodology guard.

rule.statement: Service MUST define one AppError enum implementing IntoResponse that maps every variant to an RFC 7807 problem detail with stable error codes.
rule.rationale: Without a single AppError, error handling fragments across handlers and 500-leak risk increases.

rule.statement: Shared resources MUST live behind Arc<AppState>; clone the Arc into every handler, never pass raw owned types.
rule.rationale: Cheap clones; clear ownership; multiple handlers can hold the state concurrently.

rule.statement: CPU-bound work (hashing, image processing, regex over large strings) MUST run on spawn_blocking, never inline in async handlers.
rule.rationale: Tokio worker threads are few; blocking them starves the runtime and tails latency for everyone.

rule.statement: Every new resource MUST land as one PR: model + db + service + handler + route + tests, not layer-by-layer commits.
rule.rationale: Layer-by-layer commits leave half-wired resources; a vertical slice proves end-to-end before merge.

rule.statement: Service MUST handle SIGTERM/SIGINT by stopping accept, draining in-flight requests, closing DB pool, then exiting.
rule.rationale: Without graceful shutdown Kubernetes SIGTERMs cause request drops and connection leaks.

rule.statement: If the preconditions in `Applies If` fail or any of the `Skip If` conditions is true, return `skipped` and apply no further rules.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are not universal; running them outside their fit-window wastes effort and produces wrong outputs.
